answer: Electronics led last quarter with $4.82M in revenue across 9,912 sales; the full per-category revenue, units, and sale-count breakdown is in the structured payload.

citations.snippet: SQL: SELECT p.category, SUM(f.revenue) AS total_revenue, SUM(f.units) AS total_units, COUNT(f.sale_id) AS sale_count FROM fact_sales f JOIN dim_product p ON f.product_id = p.product_id JOIN dim_date d ON f.date_id = d.date_id WHERE d.quarter = $1 AND d.year = $2 GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;  -- params: ['Q1', 2026]

routing_decision.rationale: The question is a revenue/units/volume rollup over sales grouped by product category and filtered to last quarter. The analytics-warehouse domain owns FactSales over the warehouse-sql backend and joins dim_product for category and dim_date for the quarter filter. SQL computes SUM(revenue), SUM(units), and COUNT(sale_id) deterministically via aggregate functions and GROUP BY, so it was queried directly. No other domain owns warehouse sales facts, so routing stayed single-domain.

alternative_routings.reason: Single-domain routing: warehouse-sql resolves the category group-by, the quarter filter via dim_date, and the sum/sum/count aggregations in one parameterized SELECT ... GROUP BY.
